calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-01-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Leutza in Propria Persona. Deborah Fox for and with Debra Dolch.

The Court addressed the petition for fees stating that there was not insignificant time spent, that the estate is being asked to pay fees regarding the withdrawal of counsel and appointment of Ms. Dolch and asked is the time and tasks appropriate to come out of the estate. Mr. Leutza reported that he spent time proving the lost will, that he spent time getting the executor out of the decedents apartment, that he resigned as he could not get cooperation, that he spent a lot of court time at the court's request to not leave the executor unrepresented and that it does not fall under ordinary time. The Court addressed the successor administrator's petition and asked whether it is appropriate to include a surcharge against Mr. Kernan, that it may be pursued in the future and asked if there was any downside to including it. Ms. Fox stated that it seems like an academic exercise, that there is a $191,000.00 tax lien, that he is terminally ill and in his 80's, that they performed an asset search but there are no assets, that it would not be time well spent but that they will follow the court's direction. The Court asked if it would require a separate petition or if it is within the courts purview of what is before it. Ms. Fox stated that she will defer to the court. The Court stated that the petition address surcharge but recommends that there not be one. Ms. Fox stated that it is clear that he misappropriated $50,000.00, that it is not under dispute and that they could offset by a % of the statutory commission. Ms. Dolch stated that they still have the trust administration waiting to distribute these funds. The Court took the accounting petition and the petition for allowance of extraordinary attorney's compensation under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Apr-21-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der for and with (proposed successor administrator) Debra Dolch. John Leutza for and with (former executor) Tom Kernan.

The Court stated that we are here on status of filings as well as a petition for Debra Dolch to be appointed successor administrator, that there was an issue regarding information that should have been listed in item 8, that a supplement to the petition was filed without addresses and asked if there were any objections to the appointment of Ms. Dolch. Mr. Der reported that they have the boxes of documents from the Public Administrator's office and have ordered bank records. Ms. Dolch confirmed that she will complete the accounting and stated that Mr. Kernan and the Public Administrator's office have been very cooperative. The status on filing the inventory and appraisal, accounting and delivery of all bank records as well as the motion to be relieved as counsel were taken off calendar. The Court granted the petition for letters of administration with will annexed and signed the order in open court.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing The Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Jan-13-2015 Continued To Apr-21-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for Public Administration ("PA"). John Leutza for and with Thomas Kernan. Ernest Der for Debra Dolch.

Mr. Kernan stated that he resigned as Trustee at the recommendation of Mr. Leutza, that he does not have information to do an accounting and that turned everything over to the Public Administrator. Mr. Ferdon stated that the Public Administrator absolutely does not have enough information to do an accounting for the time Mr. Kernan was Trustee. Mr. Der stated that he filed a petition to have Debra Dolch appointed as Successor Trustee in case number PTR-14-298417, that Ms. Dolch understands everything that has gone on and is ready to come in as Successor Trustee and Successor Administrator, and that he was waiting to see if the PA is willing to step aside. Mr. Ferdon stated that if Ms. Dolch files a petition to be appointed, the PA is willing step aside. Discussion was held regarding who will be the one to do the accounting, review the personal items in storage and make payment for the month of January to the storage facility. Mr. Der will file an ex parte petition for Debra Dolch to be appointed as Special Administrator. All matters were continued to April 21, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The petition for appointment of Ms. Dolch as Successor Administrator is to have the same hearing date of April 21, 2015. The hearing date of April 20, 2015 in the Trust matter is also continued to that same date (04/21/15). The Parties are to talk about the storage payment.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy S. Park. (Not Reported)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel - Civil
Off Calendar Dec-09-2014 Continued To Jan-13-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for Public Administrator. John Leutza for and with Thomas Kernan.

Mr. Ferdon stated that letters have gone to various financial institutions, that nothing has come back to send funds or verify the accounts, that he received a copy of the Trust last week and that he would hope an interested party would petition to bring the Trust under Court supervision. Mr. Leutza stated that Mr. Bell and Ms. Soares are willing to nominate someone out here to administer the estate, that he can file the nominations, a petition to remove Mr. Kernan and appoint a Successor Administration, and a petition to bring the Trust under Court supervision. Mr. Kernan waives notice of the citation for his removal. Mr. Kernan also turned over some more recent bank statements and tax notices to Mr. Ferdon in open Court. All matters were continued to January 13, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Leutza to file all of the above mentioned documents/request for appointment of someone who is qualified and represented by Counsel.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Delivery Of All Bank Records By Thomas Kernan To The Sfpa
Off Calendar Nov-12-2014 Continued To Dec-09-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Leutza for Thomas Kernan (suspended Executor). Jonathan Ferdon for Successor Administrator.

Mr. Ferdon stated that Mr. Kernan called his office to advise him that he was ill and not coming in today, that Mr. Kernan came by on Thursday with a collection of boxes, that he had three staff members working on it Friday and Monday, and that they were able to verify some accounts valued at $132,080.97. Mr. Ferdon also stated that total assets in the amount of $578,921.27 are being investigated, that there is a difference of $446,840.00 of unverified funds, that they have been unable to verify other accounts because they did not have the special letters, that they do not have a copy of the Trust and do not know the Trust parameters, and that they found an escrow statement showing that the Emeryville condominium sold for $150,000.00. In addition, Mr. Ferdon stated that there was a distribution of $125,000.00, that he asked Mr. Leutza to file a petition to bring the Trust under Court supervision, that there are several bank accounts and stocks, that there are no tax returns and that he feels Mr. Kernan will not be able to do an accounting. Mr. Leutza stated that he has a copy of the Trust, that he has no problem with provided it to Mr. Ferdon, that he spoke with Mr. Kernan about allowing the named Successor Trustee (George Bell) to come in, that Mr. Kernan has an issue with Ms. Soares coming in and that he will get a hold of George. Mr. Ferdon stated that he does not want Mr. Leutza to be relieved yet. All matters were continued to December 9, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of a petition to bring the Trust under Court supervision, for a copy of the Trust to be provided to Mr. Ferdon and for status of the assets.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Judge: Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy L. Maloney. (Not Reported)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel - Civil
Off Calendar Nov-04-2014 Continued To Nov-12-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Leutza for and with Thomas J. Kernan. Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA).

Mr. Leutza stated that George Bell declined to serve, that the "niece" has been nominated or will serve, that they have contacted attorney Alvin Buchignani, and that they have also contacted Charles DeMares who will do some light paralegal work. Mr. Kernan stated that he is not represented by counsel. The Court stated that it has not granted Mr. Leutza's motion to be relieved as counsel yet, that it was not aware that the niece was to serve, and that it had suspended Mr. Kernan's powers because he could not be bonded. The Court further stated that this matter has been continued many times and that it is inclined to appoint the SFPA. Mr. Kernan could not provide the Court with a definite answer as to where the funds were held he could only state that they were held in a BofA in North Beach. The Court presented its concerns with Mr. Kernan's responses, etc. Mr. Ferdon expressed concerns with the lack of information provided by Mr. Kernan. Mr. Kernan stated the he was sorting through all of the documents and that he needs to get the statements. Mr. Ferdon stated that Mr. Leutza should do an accounting if Mr. Kernan cannot provide one and that Mr. Kernan needs to let him know where the money is currently held. The Court continued the matters to November 12, 2014 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204 and ordered Mr. Kernan to deliver all bank records (everything) to the Court on that date. Additional discussions regarding the bank records were held. At the conclusion of the discussions, Mr. Ferdon accepted the appointment of the San Francisco Public Adminstrator (subject to a written order). The Court then ordered Mr. Kernan to deliver all of the bank records to Mr. Ferdon. Mr. Ferdon and Mr. Kernan are to confer regarding the delivery of the records to the SFPA.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy McConico. (Not Reported)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel - Civil
Off Calendar Oct-21-2014 Continued To Nov-04-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Leutza for and with Thomas Kernan. Jonathan Ferdon for Public Administrator.

The Court noted that the accounting has not been filed. Mr. Leutza stated that Mr. Kernan advised him that he is going through boxes of records to do the accounting, that the Brother of the Decedent and the Nephews and Nieces are declining to serve, that Lina Soares has been nominated and agreed to serve and that Ms. Soares will hire Charles Demaris as a fiduciary to do the leg work. The Court stated that hiring Mr. Demaris will not work, that if Ms. Soares is appointed she will have to do the work, that the Court is not willing to allow Ms. Soares to act in propria persona and that she will need to hire local counsel. Mr. Leutza stated that he will file the petition for appointed of Ms. Soares within two weeks. The Court expressed its concerns, admonished Mr. Kernan and directed him to get all of the records together. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal, status on the accounting and the motion to be relieved as counsel were continued to November 4, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel - Civil
Off Calendar Oct-07-2014 Continued To Oct-21-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Leutza for and with Thomas Kernan. Jonathan Ferdon for Public Administrator.

Mr. Leutza stated that there is a substantial investment asset in the Estate and that the condominium was sold. Mr. Kernan spoke of his medical condition, stated that he is working on getting everything in order, that the retirement account and investments are secure, that his powers were taken away and that he has not done much in the past month. The Court stated that it is in the best interest to have someone else appointed. Mr. Ferdon pointed out that Mr. Kernan still needs to do an accounting. Mr. Leutza stated the relatives back East are willing to nominate Lina Soares (Niece) as Special Administrator. Status on the inventory and appraisal, status on the accounting and the Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel were continued to October 21, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Leutza to file his petition.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Sep-09-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Leutza for and with Thomas Kernan.

Mr. Leutza stated that they applied for a bond and were rejected. The Court stated that the Trust is not before the Court but that it is worried about it. The Court suspended Mr. Kernan as Executor. The Court will ask/invite the Public Administrator to petition to be appointed as Special Administrator. Mr. Leutza stated that he was contacted by one of the Nieces and they (family) may be petitioning to be come in. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal and the Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel were continued from September 16, 2014 to October 7, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing The Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Sep-02-2014 Continued To Sep-16-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Leutza for and wth Thomas Kernan (Executor).

Mr. Leutza stated that he is asking to be relieved as Counsel due to lack of communication and failure of Mr. Kernan to perform his duties as Executor. Mr. Kernan explained why he has not communicated with Mr. Leutza, spoke of medical issues and stated that he has been communicating with the Family. Mr. Kernan also stated that the assets are at about $300,000.00 in various/several accounts with Vanguard and Transamerica, that the accounts are still in Lina's (Decedent) name, that the property has been sold and requested a 3 to 4 week continuance. Mr. Leutza stated that the property was in the Trust not the Estate. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal was continued to September 16, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Kernan to advise the Court if he will voluntarily resign. The Court ordered bond in the amount of $300,000.00. Proof of filing of bond was set for September 9, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If bond is not filed, Mr. Kernan must appear on September 9th. The Court will ask the Public Administrator to come in on the case.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jun-27-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Leutza for and with Thomas Kernan.

Mr. Leutza provided an endorsed copy of a Second Supplement to The Petition for Probate of Lost Will. There is no objection to probate of the lost will. Therefore, the will dated October 28, 2005 is admitted to probate. The Court granted the petition (subject to review of the order). Bond is not required.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.
